payload.query: If you were teaching a first year graduate course on American politics, what would you consider the six most important works essential to understanding American politics? Why? Explain the significance of each work in detail.

articles.title: Is Your Social Life Missing Something? This Conversation Is for You.
articles.abstract: Priya Parker, the author of “The Art of Gathering,” shares her advice for orchestrating more meaningful gatherings and why that matters for our civic life, as well as our social lives.
articles.snippet: Priya Parker, the author of “The Art of Gathering,” shares her advice for orchestrating more meaningful gatherings and why that matters for our civic life, as well as our social lives.
articles.byline: By Ezra Klein and Annie Galvin
keywords: The Art of Gathering: Create Transformative Meetings, Events and Experiences (Book)

articles.title: We Didn’t Ask for This Internet
articles.abstract: Cory Doctorow and Tim Wu explain why the internet failed to live up to its early promise.
articles.snippet: Cory Doctorow and Tim Wu explain why the internet failed to live up to its early promise.
articles.byline: By Ezra Klein and Annie Galvin
keywords: Enshittification: Why Everything Suddenly Got Worse and What To Do About It (Book)

articles.title: The Hip-Hop Trailblazer Who Demanded Recognition — and Pay — for Her Work
articles.abstract: Angie Stone always knew she had what it took to make it, even if the music industry did not.

articles.title: Jill Sobule’s Fans Always Made Sure She Had a Place to Perform — and to Stay

articles.title: The Psychoanalyst Who Believed Holocaust Survivors Were More Than Just Victims

articles.title: The Bonobo Who Had a Lot to Say
articles.abstract: They tried to teach his adoptive mother to communicate. Kanzi learned instead.

articles.title: D’Wayne Wiggins’s Songs Were Embraced by Everyone From Church Ladies to Biggie
